heading: Nepotism in appointment of receiver or trustee
label: 1910 Nepotism in appointment of receiver or trustee
text: Whoever, being a judge of any court of the United States, appoints as receiver, or trustee, any person related to such judge by consanguinity, or affinity, within the fourth degree—Shall be fined under this title or imprisoned not more than five years, or both.
notes: Historical and Revision NotesBased on , U.S.C., 1940 ed., Judicial Code and Judiciary (, ).section 531 of title 28Aug. 25, 1937, ch. 77750 Stat. 810Minor changes were made in phraseology.
Amendments1994— substituted “fined under this title” for “fined not more than $10,000” in last par.Pub. L. 103–322
